dialogues.dialogue: Signal Peak is the best position. Minimal atmospheric interference compared to the crash valley.
How far are we from Orbital Command?
Far enough that this relay will only get us a partial burst. But it is enough to confirm we are alive.
That will have to do.
Relay alignment at 41 percent. Atmospheric interference remains severe.
Push it anyway.
I need a higher position for alignment. I will hover above the relay dish.
Do it.
Alignment at 67 percent. Holding.
...Zegas... identify... signal weak...
...course change... not authorized... stay alive... restore chain...
What does "restore chain" mean?
Operational chain. Zegas field protocol for establishing a working extraction site.
Energy Tower, MATREX, MIRA, LIRA.
In that order. It is standard for any Zegas mining survey site. Orbital Command wants us to run the chain before they can route an extraction.
And the course change? Someone moved our armada into this planet.
That is outside my current data.
Noted.
If you can hold cover, I can run a full biological scan on the creature from here.
How long?
Thirty seconds. Do not move.
I am not moving.
Species logged. Catalog entry 1 complete. Carnivorous. Pack capable. Primary sense: motion detection. Secondary sense: vibration.
So it is listening for movement.
Yes. Standing completely still is your best defense if no cover is available.
You already know I know that.
I am adding it to the catalog for the record.
The armored creature has a patrol route. If we track it without engaging, I can map its behavior pattern.
What for?
Future encounters. Understanding how it hunts gives us an advantage.
Fine. I am following a thing that wants to eat me for science.
For survival. Science is secondary.
Running behavior pattern analysis. Hold position at the third checkpoint.
Interesting. It is not reacting to temperature or light. Only to ground contact.
Correct. It detects by vibration and ground movement. Body heat, light, and airborne scent are irrelevant.
Catalog entry 2 complete. Armored ground predator. Detection: vibration only. Heat-blind. Light-blind. Vulnerability: none confirmed at current weapon level.
None confirmed.
Not with a pulse cutter. Better weapons in a future level, possibly.
Add it to the list of reasons to survive.
Strategy note added: on foot only in armored creature zones. Bike creates detectable ground vibration at range.
Drone detected an organic heat cluster in Sector West while scanning perimeter.
Animals or plants?
Multiple small heat signatures in a compact grouping. This is a nest site.
How many?
Eight individual signatures. Egg-sized. Parents not currently detected in the immediate area.
Approach quietly. The nest material is heat-reactive — stay low and slow.
And if the parents return?
Leave. Immediately. Do not engage. Parents will be significantly larger than anything we have seen protecting a location.
Understood. Let's be fast.
Drone is scanning. Stay back. I can reach the scan range without you getting closer.
How long?
Twenty seconds. But I am picking up movement sounds from the northwest. The parents may be returning.
Faster.
Scan complete. Retreating now.
Moving. Now.
Catalog data saved. Nest coordinates marked on map. Do not return without better reasons.
Noted.
Nesting behavior logged: territorial radius approximately four hundred meters from nest center. Both parents patrol this range.
Mark every four-hundred-meter circle on the map.
Done.
Food reserves flagged. Three containers within range. Marking them.
One at a time. Scan first.
Correct. Unknown compound contamination is possible if a container seal ruptured on impact.
I will scan everything.
Survival preference logged.
(scanning) Clean. Good.
Seal is intact. Safe to open.
And the third?
Third is partially buried. Pull it clear before scanning.
Ration reserve extended. You have approximately two standard operational days of nutrition at current exertion levels.
Two days.
There is more wreckage. More containers.
Then we keep looking.
Water cartridge cache ahead. Three sealed units. One container shows a cracked seal.
Compromised how?
Cracked seal on impact. Possible interior contamination. Scan will confirm.
I will scan it.
Also: a predator is in the same corridor.
Of course it is.
Creature is on a short patrol loop. The corridor is clear for about twenty seconds on each pass.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/2 - Wait for Creature to Pass/01-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Twenty seconds is enough.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/2 - Wait for Creature to Pass/02-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/2 - Wait for Creature to Pass/02-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: If you are fast.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/2 - Wait for Creature to Pass/03-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: (scanning) First two are clean. Third one — cracked seal confirmed.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/3 - Scan and Collect Water Cartridges/01-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/3 - Scan and Collect Water Cartridges/01-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Contamination detected inside the third. Do not open it.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/3 - Scan and Collect Water Cartridges/02-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Leaving it. Taking the two.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/3 - Scan and Collect Water Cartridges/03-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/3 - Scan and Collect Water Cartridges/03-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Correct call. A contaminated water supply is worse than no supply.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-2/3 - Scan and Collect Water Cartridges/04-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Two out of three. Better than nothing.
Water reserves extended. Hydration is stable for now.
Let's not make a habit of this corridor.
Agreed.
Priority cache signal detected. Zegas corporate priority grade.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/1 - SURD Detects High-Value Cache Signal/01-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: What does that mean?
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/1 - SURD Detects High-Value Cache Signal/02-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/1 - SURD Detects High-Value Cache Signal/02-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: It means it was sealed for executives and senior geologists. The supply quality is significantly higher than field ration grade.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/1 - SURD Detects High-Value Cache Signal/03-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Finally something with upside.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/1 - SURD Detects High-Value Cache Signal/04-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/1 - SURD Detects High-Value Cache Signal/04-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: The lock needs a power cell. Use a spare crystal.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/1 - SURD Detects High-Value Cache Signal/05-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Worth it.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/1 - SURD Detects High-Value Cache Signal/06-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/1 - SURD Detects High-Value Cache Signal/06-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: This one went deep.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/2 - Clear Debris to Access Module/01-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/2 - Clear Debris to Access Module/01-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Impact buried it. Good news is nothing else found it either.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/2 - Clear Debris to Access Module/02-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Small victories.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/2 - Clear Debris to Access Module/03-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/2 - Clear Debris to Access Module/03-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Priority lock confirmed. Insert a crystal to power the bypass circuit.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/3 - Bypass Lock with Energy Crystal/01-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Zegas executive survival protocol. These caches were stocked for long-duration field operations.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/3 - Bypass Lock with Energy Crystal/02-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: One person, light exertion: twelve to fifteen days.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Food-Water-3/3 - Bypass Lock with Energy Crystal/04-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: High-grade ration packs, sealed water, medical gel. All intact.
Full cache secured. This extends your operational window considerably.
Good. Fewer chances to die between meals.
I would phrase that as improved survival margin, but your version is also accurate.
Cliff edge to the northeast. Significant elevation. I recommend a terrain scan from there.
Why?
Elevation advantage gives the drone better range and reduces atmospheric scatter. Best scan we can do without LIRA.
And it costs nothing.
Correct.
This planet is enormous.
Planet 47 is roughly 1.4 times the diameter of the Zegas home world. You were not meant to be here.
Thanks for the reminder.
Running long-range terrain scan. Range limit is approximately twelve kilometers at this altitude.
What are you looking for?
Everything. Creature densities, water sources, crash material.
Contact. Wreckage signature in the far northeastern sector. Scale is significant.
Another part of the armada?
Possibly. Scale and debris pattern do not fully match Zegas armada specifications.
You are saying it might not be ours.
I am saying the data does not confirm it is. The debris distribution pattern and structural scale are different from Zegas armada modules.
Log it. I want that on the map.
Done. Marked as unknown wreckage. We should investigate when we have a route.
After we have comms. After we have extraction. One thing at a time.
Logged in priority order.
Drone detected a water source while scanning. River system, approximately forty meters across. Sector South.
Natural water.
Do not.
I know. We scan it first.
I was going to say do not get excited. But scanning first is also correct.
It looks clean.
Run the scan before that sentence finishes.
Running.
I will overlay the biological analysis.
As expected. Unknown biological compounds. Microfauna in concentrations that would cause rapid systemic failure.
So the whole river is lethal.
Immediately and thoroughly, yes.
It looks completely safe.
Most things that kill you do.
But if we had a purification unit—
Theoretical. We do not have one.
Log the location anyway.
Done. Marked for future reference. River designated: contaminated, natural water source, Planet 47 biology.
Future reference. Meaning Level 2 or later, we might be able to use it.
Possibly. If you survive long enough to build the purification equipment.
Thanks for the vote of confidence.
I am being accurate, not optimistic.
These crystals are powering the wreckage?
Partially. Zegas catalog lists similar materials as high-density energy storage.
Similar materials?
This planet's version is undocumented.
Great. Unknown creatures, unknown crystals, no comms.
Activating crystal scanner. Stable crystals glow steady. Overloaded crystals pulse. Do not collect a pulsing crystal without stabilizing it first.
What happens if I do?
Energy discharge. Painful. Possibly fatal depending on the charge level.
Steady glow, safe. Pulsing, do not touch. Understood.
These are in almost every crack in the crash zone.
The crash may have fractured subsurface crystal formations. The planet has them in abundance.
So we landed on a power source.
Among other things. Collect the stable ones.
Insert crystals into the damaged comms panel. It needs power before it can even be assessed for repair.
Using an undocumented alien crystal to power Zegas corporate hardware.
It is compatible. I confirmed the energy frequency. Mostly.
Mostly.
The Zegas team who catalogued similar materials gave it a 94 percent compatibility rating.
And the other 6 percent?
I would not recommend testing it today.
Natural water is not worth the risk. I need sealed Zegas supplies.
Correct. Unknown biological contamination probability is unacceptable.
So I live on corporate ration packs. Perfect.
Survival preference logged.
Containers may include ration packs, sealed water cartridges, suit hydration cells, and emergency nutrient gel.
What if a container is damaged?
Scan it first. If the seal is breached, the contents may be contaminated.
Scan everything. Got it.
Manifest Bike detected. Designed for rapid traversal across mining terrain.
A vehicle crate. Finally, something useful.
It appears when summoned and hides when dismissed. Phase-fold storage, suit-linked recall.
It appears when needed and hides when not needed?
Correct. Keep it intact. You are slow without it.
Nice to know you are already judging my walking speed.
This is going to take a few minutes.
You have a pulse cutter.
I am not wasting charge on rocks.
Then you have a few minutes.
The bike needs a power cell. Insert a stable energy crystal into the crate port.
Same crystals. Every system runs on these.
Efficient design.
Or we are dangerously dependent on one resource.
That is also correct.
Syncing bike to suit. Hold still.
Synced. What does that mean?
You can summon it from anywhere in open terrain. Dismiss it by pressing and holding. It returns to phase-fold storage.
And if I dismiss it near a cliff?
It will be in storage. Not at the bottom of the cliff.
Test the summon. Then dismiss it and summon it again.
It actually works.
Did you expect otherwise?
After today? Yes.
Your equipment cannot be repaired with ration packs.
I guessed that part.
Search for ferrite mesh, conduit ore, and Zegas alloy fragments.
Mining company crashes on a planet and I still have to mine. That tracks.
Activating suit mineral scanner. Use it to identify deposits and salvage grades.
How accurate is it?
Sufficient for surface and near-surface deposits. Deeper veins require full mining equipment.
Which we do not have.
Correct. Work with what crashed.
Some of these crash craters exposed rock layers below the surface.
Crash impact does the work that mining equipment normally handles. Collect from the exposed faces.
That is enough to start repairs. The suit fabricator can process these.
Where is the fabricator?
Nearby. I am marking it. It is damaged but functional.
Movement detected ahead. Larger creature. Heavy exterior plating.
Can your drone shoot it?
Yes. Ineffectively.
Recon it is.
Sensible choice.
One armored creature. Patrol circuit covers the central passage and the east debris pile.
How long is the circuit?
Forty seconds per loop. There is a gap on the west side.
That is the way through.
Correct.
Wait for the creature to reach the east pile. Move through the west gap. On foot — the bike creates too much vibration.
On foot through armored creature territory. Perfect.
I will alert you if it changes route.
What is a signal amplifier doing out here?
It is from one of the comms modules. Still functional.
Enough to reach Orbital Command?
Combined with what we have already found, possibly.
Large hostile approaching.
Of course the first good signal rings the dinner bell.
Reminder. You are not dinner.
Tell that to the creature.
I need thirty seconds to save the transmission log. Keep it away from the relay.
Thirty seconds.
Do not let it destroy the relay. The data goes with it if it does.
Transmission log saved. We can leave.
Bike. Now.
(while riding) Is that it?
It stopped at the tree line. Territorial boundary.
They have territories.
Established behavior. We now know Signal Peak is inside that one's range.
Good to know. Where are we going?
Somewhere with less creature interest and more working Zegas infrastructure.
I need something between me and those teeth.
Crafting recommendation: emergency pulse cutter.
Is it a weapon?
Zegas classification: industrial tool.
Does it stop creatures?
Temporarily.
Then it is a weapon today.
Fabricator is damaged but repairable. Insert energy crystals to restore power.
Same crystals I just collected.
Multi-use resource. You will be depending on them for most of your systems.
You need alloy fragments, conduit minerals, and two stable energy crystals to fabricate the pulse cutter.
I have the minerals from before. I need the rest.
Alloy fragments are in the nearest cargo stack. Be fast.
Fabricating. Thirty seconds.
Pulse cutter complete.
Weapon system unlocked under emergency survival clause.
You had that clause ready fast.
You encountered carnivorous megafauna before breakfast.
Fair.
Reminder. This tool is for defense and route clearance.
Noted. I am not hunting dinner here.
Standard functions: aim, charge shot, stun pulse, and cooldown. Do not fire while cooling.
What is the cooldown period?
Eight seconds at full charge. Four at half charge.
So aim properly.
That would be ideal.
Test fire on the damaged plating to the left.
Charge level confirmed. Aim calibration complete.
Small predator approaching. This is a good test.
A test. Right.
Creature deterred. Moving away.
Okay. That works.
Return to the Type-P search route.
Transit zone ahead. Two kilometers.
I see the marker.
This is it.
Level 1 operational chain complete. Energy Tower, MATREX, MIRA, LIRA. All active.
No crew found.
No data on crew either.
Not even bodies.
That question is not answered yet.
And someone was already here. Ten to eighteen years before us.
MIRA confirmed it. Orbital Command confirmed it is real and told us to drop it.
But they are not telling us who.
Not yet.
And we are not alone. Not the creatures.
Also not yet explained.
We have a lot of questions for Level 2.
We also have a bike, a working weapon, and a drone that talks too much.
That last part is accurate.
Regret logged. Let's move.
Critical S.O.S. This is SURD. Orbital Command, can you read me?
Critical S.O.S. This is SURD. Orbital Command, respond.
Critical S.O.S. This is SURD. Armada down. Any station, respond.
Critical S.O.S. Zegas Armada 47. Does anyone copy.
Where is everyone?
This was an armada. There should be signals, bodies, something.
I need comms. If anyone survived, they will try to reach Orbital Command.
None of my comms are working. I need to find another unit.
Let me check the crashed pods.
If Orbital Command is listening, I need a transmitter strong enough to punch through this atmosphere.
The suit is damaged. I need to find the rest of it before the environment kills me.
The wreckage goes beyond the ridge.
This is too much ground to cover on foot.
I quickly need to find Food, water, comms, survivors. One thing at a time.
(quiet) What is that sound?
What the hell are these creatures?
Zegas said this planet was uninhabited.
Better hide and find comms soon.
I need food and water. I cannot find working comms anywhere.
No hunting. No unknown water. Zegas survival stock only.
Who is this? Identify yourself.
I am the player. We crashed. I cannot find comms. What is the status of the survivors?
Crash event confirmed. I lost contact with life support systems. I do not have survivor data.
I have not found anyone. Not even bodies.
Unknown variable. Do not conclude total loss without data.
I cannot establish comms with Orbital Command from this terminal.
Find a Type-P Terminal. It can hold my active node and allow portable transfer.
Where?
Three possible locations within the crash zone. I am transmitting coordinates.
And if none of them are intact?
Then keep looking. The alternative is worse.
This tower survived the crash better than anything else.
Zegas reinforced infrastructure. It was built to survive exactly this.
Surviving the crash isn't impressive anymore. Let's open it.
Security lock is damaged but active. Give me a moment.
The wildlife is going to notice the power signature when this comes on.
Faster is better.
Override sequence running. Access panel will open in ten seconds.
Tower core is open. Insert energy crystals into the power feed ports. All three ports.
Three crystals for a whole tower?
It seeds the chain reaction. The tower extracts ambient crystal energy from the local geology after that.
It keeps itself running?
Once started. Yes.
Energy Tower online. MATREX systems are now receiving power.
Good. Where is MATREX?
Sector Central. Already marked.
Then we move.
The transmission log is intact. Orbital Command is re-pinging using the log frequency.
They can reach us?
Briefly. Short burst only. Answer fast.
Signal holding. Good. Listen carefully. You cannot hold a stable comms link without active Zegas infrastructure.
Not all of it. Orbital Command is detecting a Zegas Energy Tower in this sector. Still standing.
Activate the tower. Route power to MATREX. Load MIRA. Get LIRA online. In that order.
Then we can route an extraction path.
Four systems. One sequence. I will mark the map.
You have a bike.
Energy Tower marked. Sector East. MATREX, Sector Central. MIRA, Sector Central-West. LIRA-X9, Sector North.
They are spread across the entire map.
Mining sites are not built for convenience. They are built for coverage.
Great.
Energy Tower first. Without power, MATREX cannot extract, MIRA cannot process, and LIRA cannot transmit.
And LIRA is the exit.
LIRA is the communication chain. The exit marker appears when LIRA locks on Orbital Command.
Understood. Let's move.
LIRA-X9. The last one.
Let me interface. The MIRA data needs to upload before the relay can lock on Orbital Command.
Interface port located. Connecting now.
How long for the upload?
Two minutes at this data volume.
Keep watching the perimeter.
I will notify you if anything approaches. Upload in progress.
Upload in progress. Seventy percent. Eighty.
(quiet) First time I have stood still in hours.
Ninety.
Come on.
One hundred. Upload complete.
LIRA-X9 online. Long-range relay is active.
Map exit marker placed. Orbital Command is receiving our full signal.
This is the strongest signal we have had since the crash.
And the first one with real data behind it.
MATREX station is online thanks to the tower. Power feed confirmed.
What does MATREX do exactly?
Material Extractor. Processes raw minerals, generates a certified site extraction report, and forwards the data to MIRA.
A machine that does paperwork.
A machine that tells Orbital Command the planet has extractable resources worth a rescue operation.
I stand corrected. Where is it?
Input port is blocked with crash debris. Clear it before depositing.
How much?
The port needs to be accessible. Two large pieces and some loose material.
Deposit minerals at the input port. One thousand units minimum.
All of them?
The extractor needs enough material to generate a valid site report.
That is a lot to carry around a planet full of teeth.
You have been carrying it. Now put it somewhere useful.
Extraction complete. Data packet sent to MIRA.
MIRA is next.
Correct. And the data will already be waiting when we arrive.
Efficient.
MATREX was designed to be efficient. The planet was not.
MIRA. Last one before LIRA.
(from exterior) Terminal should be live. Energy Tower power is reaching this station.
It is on. Coming in now.
Load the MATREX data packet first. It is already in your suit's relay buffer.
Uploading now.
Good. Now load the survey data from your scans.
Loading all scan data. Mineral scans, creature scans, geography scans. Everything.
MIRA builds a planetary profile. The more it has, the stronger the LIRA signal.
So everything I scanned actually mattered.
It always mattered. Now it has a destination.
What anomaly?
(entering through exterior door) That predates our armada by over a decade.
Someone was already here.
Unknown operator. MIRA cannot identify the source from extraction pattern alone.
But someone extracted minerals from this planet before we got here.
That is what the data says.
And Orbital Command told us not to investigate.
I noticed that too.
LIRA marker is active. Sector North.
How do we get past the territorial creature outside?
It moved. I will explain outside.
Survivor confirmed. LIRA operational. MIRA data received. Status report is stronger than expected.
We are reading your signal clearly. Full connection holding.
We are routing an extraction path from your current position. You need to move northeast. Transit zone. Exit coordinates sent to your suit.
Another crash site from the armada. Possibly habitable modules. Could be supplies, could be personnel.
Unknown. Move to the transit zone. Do not linger.
One more thing. The MIRA anomaly you flagged — prior extraction activity. That data matches other signals we have been tracking.
Do not investigate it on Level 1. Move to the transit zone first.
One final thing. You are not alone on that planet. Take it seriously.
Not the creatures.
Northeast. Transit zone.
Bike route is clear. I am marking the path.
Let's finish this.
Smaller one. Still not friendly.
Scan complete. Fast predator. Low armor. High bite risk.
Your summaries need fewer bite details.
Avoid the mouth.
Two options. Crouch through the side passage, or stun it and walk through the center.
Which do you recommend?
Stun costs cooldown time and crystal charge. Stealth costs nothing if it works.
If it works.
Correct qualifier.
New section of the crash field. These containers have Zegas markings I have not seen before.
Secondary cargo drop zone. Type-P search points are in this area.
Energy signature confirmed. Also confirmed: it is visible to everything within two kilometers.
How many?
Three aerial contacts. Fast. Coming from the ridge.
Those things can fly.
Glide. Technical distinction. Still dangerous.
Stun one. Scatter the rest. Keep the tower running.
Their attack pattern is dive-and-retreat. Lead the shot.
Lead the shot. Right.
They are retreating. Pack behavior. One stunned, others disengaged.
MATREX. Now.
Agreed. Before anything else finds the power signature.
MIRA station is in the next sector. There is a problem.
Of course there is.
Large territorial predator. It has claimed the MIRA site as a denning area.
Of course it has.
The good news is it is slow. The bad news is it is very large.
Can the drone distract it?
I can lure it toward the ridge while you approach from the low side. The creature reacts to movement and heat.
The drone puts out heat?
Enough at close range. I will make it worth its time.
I do not recommend thinking about that sentence too long.
Agreed. Ready when you are.
Moving toward lure point now.
I will move when the creature turns.
You will have forty seconds once it commits to the ridge.
That is enough.
Inside. Go.
(from outside) Creature is returning. I am moving to the station exterior. Safe distance.
Get clear. I have it from here.
LIRA site is ahead. I am also detecting a large apex-class signature at the site.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/1 - Apex Predator at LIRA Site/01-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: How large?
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/1 - Apex Predator at LIRA Site/02-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/1 - Apex Predator at LIRA Site/02-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Larger than anything we have encountered.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/1 - Apex Predator at LIRA Site/03-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Can we go around?
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/1 - Apex Predator at LIRA Site/04-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/1 - Apex Predator at LIRA Site/04-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: No. The LIRA tower is inside its territory. There is no clear bypass path at this range.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/1 - Apex Predator at LIRA Site/05-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Options?
Bike for speed. Drone for distraction. Weapon for direct damage. There are unstable crystal clusters near the tower site.
You want me to use the crystals as traps.
I want you to survive. The crystals are a means to that end.
Mark the cluster positions.
Marked. Approach from the west — the wind is coming from the east. It may reduce your scent signature.
It has a scent signature detection?
Most apex predators do.
Great detail to share now.
It sees you. Engaging.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/01-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Moving to the first cluster.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/02-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/02-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: I will buzz the left flank. Draw its attention.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/03-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: First hit. It is not retreating.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/04-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Second cluster. Move.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/05-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/05-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: On it.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/06-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: It is still coming.
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/07-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/07-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Third cluster. Last one in range.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/08-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: If this does not work—
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/09-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/09-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: It will work.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-Planet47-Creatures6/3 - Combat — Force the Predator Back/10-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Go. Now. LIRA before it recovers.
Agreed. Quickly. I do not know how long it stays down.
Three possible Type-P locations are marked. Search them in order. The bike will cut the travel time significantly.
Finally.
First location: the partially buried command module, northeast sector.
Type-P connection established. Transfer possible.
So you are in this node now?
Correct. I am portable but immobile.
That sounds inconvenient.
Find a compatible drone body.
Of course you need a body.
A small recon drone will be sufficient. Scout, communicate, shoot, and guide.
That is more than sufficient.

triggers.Order-Trigger: 3 - Locate and Repair the Recon Drone
triggers.trigger: Locate and Repair the Recon Drone
triggers.location$$trigger: ZC-GetDrone$$Locate and Repair the Recon Drone

dialogues.dialogue: (from suit node, muffled) Compatible drone chassis is approximately forty meters southeast. Zegas recon model. Damaged but repairable.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-SURD-Goes-Mobile/ZC-GetDrone/3 - Locate and Repair the Recon Drone/01-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Can you tell anything from in there?
dialogues.audio_file_location_female: dialogs/ZC-SURD-Goes-Mobile/ZC-GetDrone/3 - Locate and Repair the Recon Drone/02-Player-Female.mp3
dialogues.audio_file_location_male: dialogs/ZC-SURD-Goes-Mobile/ZC-GetDrone/3 - Locate and Repair the Recon Drone/02-Player-Male.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: I can interface with suit sensors. Barely. Find the drone faster.
dialogues.audio_file_location: dialogs/ZC-SURD-Goes-Mobile/ZC-GetDrone/3 - Locate and Repair the Recon Drone/03-surd.mp3

dialogues.dialogue: Portable systems online.
You can fly?
Limited altitude. Limited weapon power. Expanded attitude.
I regret giving you a body already.
Regret logged. Continuing mission support.
